genre_refs: rogues-gallery / 'most wanted' grid; magazine masthead styling
description: A designed 'rogues-gallery' graphic on a solid black background titled 'Worst of the Week.' The title is set in large white serif display type with 'Worst' largest and 'of the' inside a small red parallelogram banner, evoking a magazine masthead/awards graphic. Arranged in a grid around the central title are twelve booking/mugshot-style photographs of men of varied ages and ethnicities, each in a small rounded-rectangle frame with a dark caption bar beneath naming an alleged or convicted crime. The captions read: 'Child Molestation', 'Smuggling Aliens', 'Child Rape', 'Firearm Possession', 'Child Sexual Conduct', 'Manslaughter', 'Fondling a Child', 'Manslaughter', 'Child Molestation', 'Assault and Battery', 'Human Smuggling', 'Cocaine Distribution' (one image at lower right is a blurred/low-resolution thermal-looking grayscale face). Bottom left has a red banner with white text 'ice.gov/WoW'; bottom right shows the gray DHS seal and the 'ICE' wordmark. The propaganda technique compiles severe-sounding charges (heavy on child sex offenses) into a single shareable card to portray immigrants en masse as dangerous criminals; the tweet explicitly asks viewers to share the 'criminal alien' information. Target audience: the general public / supporters, intended for viral sharing.
notable_text: Worst of the Week; Child Molestation; Smuggling Aliens; Child Rape; Firearm Possession; Manslaughter; Human Smuggling; Cocaine Distribution; ice.gov/WoW
